tool.name: Edit COCO Annotation
tool.description: Filter and rename tracks in a COCO annotation file
tool.command: mkdir -p outputs && python3 '$__tool_directory__/edit_coco_annotation.py' --annotations '$annotations' --mode '$mode.mode' #if $mode.mode == "keep" #for $track in $mode.entries #set $rename_val = str($track.rename).strip() if str($track.rename).strip() not in ('', 'None') else "" #set $fmin_val = str($track.frame_min) if str($track.frame_min) not in ('', 'None') else "" #set $fmax_val = str($track.frame_max) if str($track.frame_max) not in ('', 'None') else "" --entry '${track.track_ids}|${rename_val}|${fmin_val}|${fmax_val}' #end for #elif $mode.mode == "remove" #for $track in $mode.entries #set $fmin_val = str($track.frame_min) if str($track.frame_min) not in ('', 'None') else "" #set $fmax_val = str($track.frame_max) if str($track.frame_max) not in ('', 'None') else "" --entry '${track.track_ids}|${fmin_val}|${fmax_val}' #end for #else #for $track in $mode.entries --entry '${track.track_ids}|${track.name}' #end for #end if

param.label: COCO annotation file
param.help: JSON annotation file produced by SAM3 or any other COCO-format tool.

param.label: Mode
param.help: Choose what to do with the track IDs listed below.
param.option: Keep - keep only listed IDs (remove all others)
Remove - remove only listed IDs (keep all others)
Rename - keep all tracks, rename listed IDs

repeat.name: entries
repeat.title: Track to keep
repeat.help: All track IDs not listed here will be removed from the output.

param.name: track_ids
param.label: Track IDs
param.help:
Comma-separated list (5,7,25), for a range use a dash '-' (12-20), or combine both (3,7,12-20,25)
⚠ First track_id is zero "0"

validator.message: Use digits, commas and dashes only, e.g. '3,7,12-20'.

param.name: rename
param.label: Rename (optional)
param.help: New category name for these tracks. Leave empty to keep the original name.

param.name: frame_min
param.label: Frame min (optional)
param.help: Keep annotations from this frame number onwards.

param.name: frame_max
param.label: Frame max (optional)
param.help: Keep annotations up to this frame number, inclusive.

repeat.name: entries
repeat.title: Track to remove
repeat.help: All track IDs not listed here will be kept unchanged.

param.name: track_ids
param.label: Track IDs
param.help:
Comma-separated list (5,7,25), for a range use a dash '-' (12-20), or combine both (3,7,12-20,25)
⚠ First track_id is zero "0"

validator.message: Use digits, commas and dashes only, e.g. '3,7,12-20'.

param.name: frame_min
param.label: Frame min (optional)
param.help: Remove annotations only from this frame onwards (leave empty to remove all frames).

param.name: frame_max
param.label: Frame max (optional)
param.help: Remove annotations only up to this frame, inclusive (leave empty to remove all frames).

repeat.name: entries
repeat.title: Track to rename
repeat.help: All tracks are kept. Listed IDs get a new category name.

param.name: track_ids
param.label: Track IDs
param.help:
Comma-separated list (5,7,25), for a range use a dash '-' (12-20), or combine both (3,7,12-20,25)
⚠ First track_id is zero "0"

validator.message: Use digits, commas and dashes only, e.g. '3,7,12-20'.

param.name: name
param.label: New category name
param.help: New category name assigned to every track in this group.

tool.help:
=============================== Edit COCO Annotation ===============================
Filter and rename tracks in a COCO annotation file produced by SAM3 (or any COCO-format tool).
------------------- Modes -------------------
**Keep** — Only the track IDs you list survive; all others are deleted. Each entry can optionally rename the tracks and/or restrict them to a frame window.
**Remove** — Only the track IDs you list are deleted; everything else is kept. Each entry can optionally restrict removal to a frame window.
**Rename** — Every track is kept, but the listed IDs receive a new category name.
--------------------- COCO field reference ---------------------
- Track IDs are read from the ``track_id`` field of each annotation entry (``annotations[].track_id``). - Frame numbers are read from the ``frame_index`` field of each image entry (``images[].frame_index``).
------------------- Notes -------------------
- Annotations without a ``track_id`` are always kept unchanged. - Images with no remaining annotations are removed from the output. - Unused categories are cleaned up automatically. - If an ID appears in multiple entries, the last entry wins.
